briefs.title: BC exempts North Coast Transmission Line Project from standard utility approval process
briefs.summary: A new BC regulation exempts BC Hydro and its co-ownership partners from needing a 'certificate of public convenience and necessity' (a government permit normally required before building major utility infrastructure) for the North Coast Transmission Line Project. This covers new 500 kilovolt power lines running from Prince George to Terrace, plus related upgrades, allowing construction to proceed faster without the usual regulatory review steps.
briefs.industries: Natural Resources, Construction & Development, Transportation & Logistics
